Title: Grounded LVLM Hallucination Mitigation

Abstract:
Large vision-language models (LVLMs) can hallucinate objects that are not
supported by an image. We study hallucination mitigation using retrieved visual
evidence and an uncertainty gate.

Method:
The method retrieves image-region evidence, estimates answer uncertainty, and
routes uncertain examples through a verification stage before decoding.

Experiments:
We evaluate on POPE and MMHal-Bench against an unmodified LVLM, prompt-only
mitigation, and self-consistency baselines. Metrics include accuracy,
hallucination rate, latency, and token cost.

Limitations:
The uncertainty gate can be miscalibrated under domain shift, and the
verification stage increases inference cost.
